questions.question: Compare arrays and linked lists. When would you prefer one over the other?
keyPoints: Array: O(1) access, O(n) insert/delete, contiguous memory
Linked List: O(n) access, O(1) insert/delete with pointer, non-contiguous
Array: better cache performance, no pointer overhead
Linked List: dynamic size, no shifting required
Prefer Array: frequent access, known size, cache-critical
Prefer Linked List: frequent insert/delete, unknown size, implementing stacks/queues

questions.question: Write a C function to count the number of nodes in a linked list.
keyPoints: Initialize counter to 0
Traverse using while loop until NULL
Increment counter for each node
Return the count

questions.question: Explain the node structure of a linked list with a diagram.
keyPoints: Node has two parts: data and next pointer
Data holds the actual value
Next pointer stores address of next node
Last node's next points to NULL
Draw diagram showing node structure and linked nodes

questions.question: Write a function to search for a given value in a linked list and return its position.
keyPoints: Traverse from head
Compare each node's data with key
Track position using counter
Return position if found, -1 otherwise

questions.question: List and explain three real-world applications of linked lists.
keyPoints: Music player playlist - prev/next navigation
Browser history - back/forward buttons
Undo functionality - stack of operations using linked list
Memory allocation - free list in OS
Polynomial representation - terms as nodes
Hash table chaining - collision handling
